title: Uncertainty and traffic-volatility features as pit-pressure signals
summary: F1 simulation literature emphasizes lap-time variance, pit-stop duration variance, traffic, and undercut conditions as strategic drivers.
rationale: F1 simulation literature emphasizes lap-time variance, pit-stop duration variance, traffic, and undercut conditions as strategic drivers. Your baseline uses mean-like pace/degradation features but not how unstable or risky the local race state is.
implementation_hint: Compute past-only rolling std/MAD of `LapTime_Delta`, stint residuals, and position changes within Race-Year/Driver; add same-lap field pit-rate, same-lap pace dispersion, and nearby-position pace ranks as traffic-volatility proxies. Interact these with `LapsRemaining_Est` and compound.
expected_effect: Should capture moments when teams pit because the current state is unstable or because an undercut window is opening, even if tyre-life thresholds alone do not fire.
risk: Same-lap aggregates can leak if they implicitly use future ordering or post-pit effects from other cars on that lap; build them only from contemporaneous fields and test with purged race splits.
